steps.title: Identify the rhetorical setup
steps.content: The passage opens with rhetorical questions inviting readers to assume plants cannot grow on Mars, then immediately shifts with “but researchers in Denmark recently showed” This contrast—expectation versus reality—reveals the passage's central purpose: to present a finding that contradicts what readers would initially assume.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
B correctly states the passage presents “a surprising finding about plants grown in soil intended to be similar to Martian soil.” The passage's entire structure—from expectation-setting rhetorical questions, through the “but” pivot, to concrete evidence of unexpected success (30% versus 4% germination rates)—is designed to present this counterintuitive result.

A, C, and D either misidentify the focus (simulation methods, water's role, soil composition analysis) or fail to capture the “surprising” element that drives the passage.

B is correct.
